subject: Math

game_theme.title: Classify Two-Dimensional Figures
game_theme.mission_intro: Use classify two-dimensional figures by properties and shape hierarchies to solve real Grade 5 math missions.

lesson.objective: classify two-dimensional figures by properties and shape hierarchies
lesson.concept_explanation: classify two-dimensional figures by properties and shape hierarchies means reading the situation, matching it to a visual model, and using precise Grade 5 math language before answering.
lesson.common_misconception: A common mistake is focusing on one number or one visible feature without checking the labels, units, axes, or attributes that give the problem meaning.
lesson.real_world_connection: Students, scientists, designers, builders, and data readers use these skills to describe and solve real problems clearly.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use the model, name the important labels, and answer with accurate polygons, triangles, quadrilaterals, rectangles, rhombi, squares, attributes, hierarchy.

page_audio.text: This mini lesson introduces classify two-dimensional figures by properties and shape hierarchies. The visual model is part of the thinking, not decoration. Say the important labels aloud, then solve with the correct math language.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

worked_examples.title: Classify a square
worked_examples.text: A square is a rectangle because it has four right angles. It is also a rhombus because it has four equal sides.

page_audio.text: Shape hierarchies are nested. A square belongs inside rectangles and rhombi because it has both sets of properties. It also belongs to quadrilaterals and polygons.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

worked_examples.title: Sort by attributes
worked_examples.text: Sort figures by properties such as side count, parallel sides, equal sides, and right angles.

page_audio.text: Classifying shapes means checking properties, not just appearance. Size and tilt do not change the category. Use attributes like sides, angles, and parallel lines.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

Use the visual model to check each part before answering.
Explain why the answer matches the units, graph, or attributes.

story.text: Welcome to Classify Two-Dimensional Figures. In this mission, you will use classify two-dimensional figures by properties and shape hierarchies to solve problems that feel like real classroom and world tasks. Watch how the labels, units, and visual model tell you what to do next.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches classify two-dimensional figures by properties and shape hierarchies. Read the model before calculating, because the model shows what each number or attribute means. Use precise math language: polygons, triangles, quadrilaterals, rectangles, rhombi, squares, attributes, hierarchy.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: On the Watch page, slow down and follow one complete example. First name what the question asks, then connect each label in the visual to the operation or property. Finally, say the answer in a complete math sentence.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: On the Demo page, build the reasoning with support. Notice where common mistakes happen, such as reversing coordinates, converting the wrong direction, or using only one shape attribute. The model is here to help you catch the mistake early.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This Practice page lets you try the skill yourself. Tap Read Question before you answer so the measurement, coordinate, or geometry words are spoken clearly. Then use the visual and choose or type the answer carefully.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This Challenge page may combine ideas or ask for a deeper explanation. Tap Read Question, study the visual, and decide which information matters most. If the answer has a unit, coordinate, or shape category, include it.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This Checkpoint page checks what you can do independently. Tap Read Question, solve without rushing, and compare your answer to the model. Your response should show that you understand the classify two-dimensional figures by properties and shape hierarchies.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: You reached the badge page for Classify Two-Dimensional Figures. Think about the model that helped most and the misconception you avoided. This badge celebrates careful reasoning, not just a quick answer.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: The profile page saves your growth for Classify Two-Dimensional Figures. Review your strengths, then continue to the Skill Practice Center for focused levels or Mixed review. Choose Practice This Skill when you want more questions with Read Question support.
profile.label: Read This Page
